commit.message:
kernel: enable BPF_SYSCALL and CGROUP_BPF for Docker cgroup v2

Docker/runc uses bpf_prog_query(BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE) to manage device
access under cgroup v2. Without CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL=y the syscall is not
compiled in and the call returns ENOSYS, causing every container start
to fail with:

  error setting cgroup config for procHooks process:
  bpf_prog_query(BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE) failed: function not implemented

The base kernel has CONFIG_BPF=y but CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL was explicitly
disabled. Enable it along with CONFIG_CGROUP_BPF which gates the cgroup
device-filtering program type.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use received HTTP bytes (not decrypted) for EOF detection in windowed stream

window_got (decrypted bytes delivered to caller) understates the actual
server response when block_skip > 0 — i.e. when a TCP drop occurred at
a non-AES-block-boundary offset and the reconnect re-requests from an
earlier aligned position.  In that case window_got < _RANGE_WINDOW even
though the server sent a full window, causing the loop to return early
and truncate the stream.

Fix: compute received = window_got + block_skip (actual HTTP bytes from
the server) and use that for the EOF guard instead of window_got.

Add test_get_audio_stream_continues_after_non_block_boundary_drop:
- patches _RANGE_WINDOW to 32 bytes, 50-byte file
- drops at byte 17 (non-block boundary), reconnect delivers full 32-byte
  window from block_start=16, second window delivers remaining 2 bytes
- verifies all 50 bytes are received and exactly 3 HTTP requests are made

Ref: https://github.com/music-assistant/server/pull/3237#discussion_r2894243866

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix TLS checkptr violation in test

The TestBlockInitialization test was creating a Go slice on the stack
and passing its address as InitData. This violates Go's checkptr rules
because:

1. Converting &slice[0] to uintptr and storing it breaks the
   'immediate use' pattern required for safe uintptr→unsafe.Pointer
2. The Go runtime's race detector correctly flags this as potentially
   unsafe pointer arithmetic

Fix: Use mmap.Map() to allocate the init data in kernel memory instead
of Go-managed memory. This matches real-world usage where InitData
points to a PT_LOAD segment from an ELF file.

The TLS implementation code in tls.go was correct - only the test
needed fixing. Added clarifying comment to Module.InitData field.

Fixes: go test -race ./internal/tls/ now passes
Tests: All tests pass with race detector enabled

commit.message:
feat: implement graceful shutdown for the HTTP server (#1608)

- [x] Update `cmd/manael/main.go` to use `http.Server` with graceful
shutdown via `signal.NotifyContext()` and `srv.Shutdown()`
- [x] Add `ReadHeaderTimeout: 10 * time.Second` to the server for
security best practice
- [x] Build and verify the changes compile correctly (go vet exit 0)
- [x] Code review passed
- [x] CodeQL security scan: 0 alerts
- [x] Restore `website/go.mod` — accidentally deleted `require
github.com/google/docsy v0.10.0 // indirect` line has been restored

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Implement graceful shutdown for the HTTP
server</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, the application starts the server using
`http.ListenAndServe()` in `cmd/manael/main.go`. When the process is
terminated (e.g., via `SIGTERM` or `SIGINT`), it shuts down abruptly,
dropping any in-flight HTTP requests.
> 
> To improve reliability and follow modern Go best practices, we should
implement a graceful shutdown mechanism.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Update `cmd/manael/main.go` to use `http.Server.Shutdown()` in
combination with `signal.NotifyContext()` (introduced in Go 1.16). This
will allow the server to finish processing active requests before
exiting.
> 
> ### Example Implementation
> ```go
> package main
> 
> import (
> 	"context"
> 	"errors"
> 	"log"
> 	"net/http"
> 	"os/signal"
> 	"syscall"
> 	"time"
> )
> 
> func main() {
> 	// ... (setup handler, etc.)
> 
> 	srv := &http.Server{
> 		Addr:              conf.httpAddr,
> 		Handler:           handler,
> 		ReadHeaderTimeout: 10 * time.Second, // Security best practice
> 	}
> 
> ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt,
syscall.SIGTERM)
> 	defer stop()
> 
> 	go func() {
> 		log.Printf("Starting server on %s", conf.httpAddr)
> if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && !errors.Is(err,
http.ErrServerClosed) {

feat: add file upload to session working directory (#113)

## Summary

Upload files from your phone (or any browser) directly into a terminal
session's working directory.

### Features

- **Upload endpoint**: `POST /api/sessions/:id/upload` — buffers raw
file body and writes to the session's cwd with optional `X-Target-Dir`
override
- **Command Palette action**: "Upload files" under Session category
(Ctrl+K)
- **Confirmation modal**: shows file list with sizes, destination
directory with folder browser
- **Client-side validation**: files exceeding 10 MB shown in red with
Upload button disabled
- **Filename sanitization**: strips path traversal sequences, control
characters, collapses whitespace; atomic dedup via exclusive-create
(`wx` flag) with retry on collision
- **Path security**: rejects relative `X-Target-Dir`, validates absolute
paths, containment check ensures writes stay inside target directory
- **Toast notification**: "3 files uploaded to /path/to/dir" on
completion

### Testing

- 14 new tests covering happy path, dedup, path traversal, invalid
filenames, empty body, oversized files, custom target dir, non-existent
dir, relative dir, non-directory target, read-only dir (skipped on
Windows)
- All tests pass

### Docs

- Updated `docs/api.md`, `docs/usage-guide.md`, `docs/index.md`,
`README.md`, `landing/index.html`

Closes #110

commit.message:
Complete __tls_get_addr implementation for TLS support

Implement C-callable __tls_get_addr function to enable General Dynamic (GD)
TLS access model for dynamically loaded libraries. This allows libraries
with __thread variables to be loaded and executed successfully.

Changes:

1. internal/tls/tls_get_addr.go:
   - Use purego.NewCallback to create C-callable function pointer
   - Replace placeholder return value with actual callback registration
   - Enable C code to call Go's GetTLSAddr implementation

2. loader/loader.go:
   - Fix TLS initialization data pointer calculation
   - Account for page alignment offset when mapping PT_TLS segments
   - Add leading offset to seg.Addr to get correct TLS data address
   - Fixes bug where tls_counter was reading wrong memory location

3. dl/dl_test.go:
   - Enhance TestTLSLibraryParsing to actually call TLS functions
   - Add tests for get_tls_counter() and increment_tls_counter()
   - Verify TLS variables are correctly initialized and modifiable

4. ROADMAP.md:
   - Update TLS Support section to reflect __tls_get_addr completion
   - Move __tls_get_addr from "Not Yet Implemented" to "Completed"
   - Document remaining limitations (code sequence relocations, multi-threading)
   - Update current status to reflect working GD TLS model

Key Fix:
The TLS initialization data pointer was incorrectly calculated because
seg.Addr is page-aligned down, but we were treating it as if it pointed
to the segment's actual vaddr. The fix adds the "leading" offset
(ph.Vaddr - alignedVAddr) to correctly locate TLS data within the mapped
segment.

Test Results:
- All tests pass with CGO_ENABLED=0
- TLS counter correctly initialized to 42
- TLS variables can be read and modified through functions
- No complexity or duplication regressions

Limitations:
- Single-threaded only (uses pseudo thread ID = 1)
- R_X86_64_TLSGD, TLSLD, GOTTPOFF not yet supported (code rewriting needed)
- Full multi-threading would require gettid() syscall integration

commit.message:
fix: auto-reconnect stale SMTP connections before sending

The SMTP connection was established once at startup and held open
indefinitely. When the server closed the idle connection, all
subsequent sends failed with 421 timeout / SMTPServerDisconnected
errors and never recovered.

Changes:
- Add _ensure_connected() that probes with NOOP and reconnects
- Add _send_with_retry() with configurable MAX_SEND_RETRIES
- Both send methods now auto-reconnect on stale connections
- Update unit tests for new retry/reconnect behavior
- Add test_send_retries_on_stale_connection regression test

feat(patterns): fractal-worktree + backprop-merge patterns (TinyAGI/fractals port)

Source: https://github.com/TinyAGI/fractals (MIT)
Decision: port 2 key innovations to SF's Python/AgentExecutor stack instead
of running external Claude/Codex CLI.

Pattern #16 — fractal-worktree (platform/patterns/impls/fractal_worktree.py):
- Phase 1 PLAN: LLM classify gate (ATOMIC vs COMPOSITE) before decomposing
- Recursive decompose() builds a leaf tree (max_depth=3, max_children=5)
- Phase 2 EXECUTE: leaf tasks run in parallel, each in an isolated git worktree
  (git worktree add) to prevent parallel agent file conflicts
- Graceful degradation: if no git repo → plain parallel execution

Pattern #17 — backprop-merge (platform/patterns/impls/backprop_merge.py):
- Same PLAN phase as fractal-worktree
- After leaf execution: merge agent synthesizes results bottom-up
  leaf1+leaf2 → merge(composite1) → ... → merge(root)
- Resolves conflicts between parallel agents; produces coherent final output
- Source: TinyAGI/fractals roadmap "Backpropagation (merge agent)"

Wiring:
- engine.py: dispatch cases for "fractal-worktree" and "backprop-merge" types
- store.py: PatternDef seeds for both patterns
- registry.yaml: fractal-worktree module card with source + rationale comment

Tests: 18 offline tests in tests/test_fractal_patterns.py
(parse_subtasks, classify prompt, FractalNode tree, git detection, merge prompt)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
